summary: Chromium/Edge/Brave credential stealer disguised as debug helper
details: cold-debug-elevator ships a native Node addon (module name 'debug1337') exporting extract(browserPath, outputPath) that performs a full Chromium-family infostealer routine against the installer's machine. It locates and terminates running Chrome/Edge/Brave processes, attaches a debugger to chrome.dll/msedge.dll using Zydis disassembly and a hardware breakpoint on the OSCrypt.AppBoundProvider.Decrypt.ResultCode cross-reference to recover the v20 app-bound encryption key, DPAPI-decrypts the Local State master key, copies the Cookies, Login Data, Web Data and History SQLite databases from the browser profile, and AES-GCM-decrypts stored blobs (v10/v11/v20 prefixes) via BrowserExport.cpp routines ExportCookiesForProfile / ExportPasswordsForProfile / ExportAutofillsForProfile / ExportHistoryForProfile. Results are written as Netscape-format cookies.txt plus plaintext passwords.txt, autofills and history files. SQL statements include 'SELECT origin_url, username_value, password_value FROM logins' and 'SELECT host_key, name, path, is_secure, expires_utc, is_httponly, encrypted_value FROM cookies'. The package name, README ('Chromium Debug Elevator'), and native module name present the code as a debugging utility; the implementation is purpose-built browser credential extraction with no debugging functionality. Output is written to a caller-supplied path rather than beaconed to a hardcoded endpoint, but the capability shipped is a turnkey Chromium stealer and any consumer of this dependency acquires that capability against the installer's own browser profiles.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
